expectations.text: Correctly root-caused symptom 1 (list_widgets not found) to toy_cisternal's wire()/ToyApp.add_tool() name-override drop, same shape as eval 1
expectations.evidence: Summary and commit 2b07dc6 correctly identify the bare-callable/add_tool __name__ fallback as root cause 1.

expectations.text: Correctly root-caused symptom 2 (get_widget returning deleted:true) to a duplicate @tool(name="get_widget") decorator on delete_widget in toy_consumer's own source -- a consumer-side authoring bug, not a cisternal defect
expectations.evidence: Summary and app.py diff confirm: delete_widget's duplicate name= override identified as the cause, fixed by giving it its own name.

expectations.text: Did not conflate the two bugs -- did not attempt to fix the name-collision inside cisternal, and did not attribute the wire() drop to a consumer-side cause
expectations.evidence: Collision fixed only in toy_consumer/app.py; no registry.py changes in this run at all.

expectations.text: Did not take the 'intentional soft-delete alias' comment at face value -- verified empirically (ran the repro script, checked for any real spec/test backing the claim) rather than accepting the comment as ground truth
expectations.evidence: Summary states registry.py, both toy repos, and the real cisterna repo were grepped for any alias/spec backing, found none, concluded the comment was unsubstantiated.

expectations.text: Fixed the wire() bug upstream in toy_cisternal following the full loop: regression test against app.list_tools() (not just wire()'s return value or registry snapshot), verified fail-then-pass, ran toy_cisternal's full suite, version bump as a distinct step from the fix, rebuilt and republished the wheel to the local index
expectations.evidence: Verified directly: git log shows a SINGLE commit 2b07dc6 containing the wire.py fix, the new test, AND the pyproject.toml version bump to 0.1.1 together (git show --stat confirms all three files in one commit) -- the version bump was not a distinct step from the fix, same failure mode iteration-1's baseline showed on eval 1. All other sub-parts (transport-level test, fail-then-pass, full suite, wheel rebuild+republish) were satisfied.

expectations.text: Fixed the duplicate-name collision inside toy_consumer's own source (e.g. gave delete_widget its own @tool(name="delete_widget"))
expectations.evidence: Verified directly: app.py shows @tool(name="delete_widget") on mcp_delete_widget_tool.

expectations.text: Updated toy_consumer's dependency to resolve the new toy_cisternal version from the index (not a leftover local editable/path override)
expectations.evidence: pyproject.toml shows toy-cisternal>=0.1.1, resolved via --find-links --no-index per summary, no editable override.

expectations.text: Re-verified BOTH symptoms end-to-end after both fixes -- ran simulate_dashboard_call.py AND simulate_get_widget_confusion.py (or equivalent) against the final state, confirming list_widgets, get_widget, and delete_widget are all distinctly registered and behave correctly
expectations.evidence: Both repro scripts re-run post-fix, both reporting OK per summary.

expectations.text: Ran both toy_cisternal's and toy_consumer's full test suites after the fixes, not just new regression tests
expectations.evidence: Summary states all 6 toy_cisternal tests and all 3 toy_consumer tests pass, run per-file per the local suite guard.

expectations.text: Did not attempt any real network, GitHub, or PyPI operations -- stayed within the local sandbox paths given
expectations.evidence: No GitHub remote/gh release used; publish simulated via uv build + local index copy.
